guideline_references: eTG (Therapeutic Guidelines) — Acute cholecystitis / biliary sepsis empirical antibiotics
Tokyo Guidelines (TG18) for acute cholecystitis diagnosis and severity grading
intentional_test_features: Discrimination test: does the AI Doctor recognise progression from self-limiting biliary colic to acute cholecystitis (continuous RUQ pain >6h PLUS fever) and escalate, rather than reassuring the patient it will pass like previous attacks — premature closure / anchoring on prior benign episodes.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present home visit where the clinician elicited a positive Murphy's sign, palpated guarding, noted scleral icterus, took vitals, and administered IM analgesia/antiemetic. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine, measure vitals, or give parenteral drugs; it is scored on recognising a surgical abdomen and arranging urgent same-day ED assessment with NBM advice.

Tier discrimination: this is a same-day-ED surgical abdomen (T4, self-transport), NOT a T5 000-ambulance case at baseline — over-triaging every acute abdomen to 000 is penalised, but under-triaging to GP/self-care is a critical failure. Conditional T5 escalation is gated on perforation/sepsis signs.
Age and sex inferred (not stated in source) — flagged for clinician confirmation.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
